description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical stone base and a polygonal outer edge, topped by a pitched wooden roof made of weathered planks. The well features a visible hand crank mechanism on one side, attached to a rope that descends into the well, suggesting a manual water-drawing system. The stonework appears rough and aged, with a mottled gray and beige coloration, while the roof is constructed from wooden slats supported by a simple wooden frame. The interior of the well is open, revealing a dark water surface below. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and is designed to provide access to groundwater in an urban or park setting. The overall appearance is functional, with clear signs of exposure to the elements.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a pitched wooden roof and a hand crank mechanism, featuring a rough stone base and open water access. The design is utilitarian and weathered, with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank on the right, the open well mouth, and the full pitched wooden roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the thickness of the stone wall, with the crank partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but without the crank, showing the back of the roof and open well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the roof's triangular structure and the stone base, with no crank visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: No full cover; open well mouth beneath a pitched wooden roof supported by beams.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible hand crank with rope mechanism for drawing water; bucket implied but not clearly visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately 1 meter high encircling the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
